description: Drive a WLED LED strip from video content playing in Jellyfin
name: WLED TV
overview: Samples pixel colours along the edges of the playing video and sends them to a WLED controller in real time, creating an immersive edge-lighting effect that reacts to the content on screen.

versions.changelog:
## Changes

**Perf: increase batch size from 32 to 54 LEDs (9 to 5 batches per frame)**

Batch sizes 48 through 62 were tested against the live ESP8266 device (270 LEDs, 17 KB free heap):

- 48, 50, 54, 56: OK
- 58, 60, 62: error 9 (ArduinoJson buffer exceeded)

Maximum safe batch size is 56. Using 54 for a 4-LED safety margin, and because 270 / 54 = 5 exactly (five equal batches, no partial chunk).

This reduces WebSocket messages per frame from 9 to 5, cutting ArduinoJson parse operations from ~225 to ~125 per second at 25 fps — a 44% reduction in ESP8266 processing load.

## Changes

**Fix: restore working LED updates from v1.1.4.0**

Every version from v1.1.5.0 to v1.1.7.0 broke LED colour updates for one of three reasons:

- v1.1.5.0: binary WebSocket frames are silently ignored by WLED (WebSocket is JSON-only)
- v1.1.6.0: a single 270-LED JSON frame exceeds the ESP8266 ArduinoJson buffer (error 9 on every tick)
- v1.1.7.0: setTimeout pacing - tick() shortens the next interval by the elapsed processing time, so the next sendColors fires and cancels still-pending timers before they run, resulting in no LED updates

This release restores v1.1.4.0's synchronous burst approach (all 9 batches of 32 LEDs sent in one JS tick) with one change: on/bri is sent once per WebSocket connection instead of every frame. The per-frame on/bri was the source of the TCP congestion in v1.1.4.0.

## Changes

**Fix: LED freeze / catch-up congestion (root cause analysis and correct fix)**

Every release since 1.1.4.0 failed for an explainable reason:

- **1.1.5.0 (binary frames)**: WLED's WebSocket interface only accepts JSON text frames. Binary frames are silently ignored. The on/bri JSON worked (LEDs turned on to solid color), but the binary color data was dropped.
- **1.1.6.0 (single 270-LED JSON)**: error 9 on every frame. 270 hex strings exceeds the ESP8266 ArduinoJson document buffer (~32 entries max, confirmed empirically). on/bri worked, color data was silently dropped.
- **1.1.4.0 (32-LED batching)**: correct data, but all 9 batches were fired synchronously in one JavaScript tick. This flooded the ESP8266 TCP receive buffer and caused the freeze-then-catch-up congestion.

**This release** keeps 32-LED hex-string batching (the only approach that works on ESP8266) but spreads the 9 batch sends across 80% of the tick window using setTimeout. At 25 fps (40 ms ticks) the spacing is 4 ms per batch, giving the device ~3 ms of idle time between each ~1-2 ms JSON parse. The TCP buffer never fills up.

## Changes

Diagnostic baseline: single JSON hex-string frame for all LEDs, no batching, no binary.

Sends all configured LED colours in one JSON message using hex strings (e.g. \\FF0000\\). The on/bri power frame is still sent only once per WebSocket connection rather than every frame.

If error 9 appears on the device the ArduinoJson buffer limit is confirmed real. If it works cleanly this establishes that the 32-LED batching in 1.1.4.0 was unnecessary.

## Changes

- **Fix: TCP congestion and LED freeze on ESP8266 devices**

  Version 1.1.4.0 sent 9 batched JSON frames per tick. At 10 fps this produced ~100 JSON parse operations per second on the ESP8266, starving the LED output and WiFi stack. The result was LEDs freezing after a few seconds and catching up when playback stopped.

  This release replaces JSON batching with WLED binary live-data protocol (DRGB format): one 812-byte binary frame instead of nine ~300-byte JSON frames. No ArduinoJson allocation, no JSON parsing overhead on the device.

  Binary frame layout: byte 0x02 (DRGB), byte 0x05 (5-second realtime timeout), then R,G,B triplets. The plugin sends a v:true probe on connect and waits for the WLED version response before switching to binary, so the mock server path is unaffected.

- on/bri is now sent once per WebSocket connection rather than every frame.

## Changes

- **Fix: WLED JSON API compatibility with ESP8266 devices**
  - LED colours are now sent as 6-character hex strings (`RRGGBB`) instead of `[r,g,b]` sub-arrays, matching the documented WLED `i`-field format
  - Colours are batched into groups of 32 per WebSocket message to stay within the ArduinoJson document buffer limit on ESP8266 (error 9 was observed at 64+ LEDs per message on a device with 17 KB free heap)
  - Subsequent batches use the `[startIndex, hex, ...]` prefix so each chunk is placed at the correct strip position without touching other LEDs
  - The power-on frame (`on`/`bri`) continues to be sent as a separate leading message, as combining it with `i` in a single request is a documented no-op when turning on from an off state
Raise mostly-black frame threshold from 5% to 6.5% to suppress flickering on denser credit frames.
Skip letterbox/pillarbox crop on mostly-black frames to prevent credits from blasting white LEDs.

Fix mock-logging checkbox not rendering (emby-checkbox polyfill skipped it).

Device dropdown now filtered: hides Jellyseerr entries, devices inactive > 2 weeks, and devices last used by login-screen-hidden users.

WebGL: replace ctx.drawImage cross-context copy with gl.readPixels — eliminates the GPU pipeline flush causing ~800 ms per frame on WebOS.

Fixes mock-logging checkbox not rendering in settings.

Performance: 1/4-scale capture and single getImageData call per frame.

Reduces WebGL GPU readback from ~6 MB to ~400 KB; eliminates per-region getImageData calls. Expected to bring refresh rate from ~800 ms to ~50-100 ms on WebOS.

Also fixes the mock-logging checkbox not rendering in the settings page.

Add WebGL capture method setting and mock debug logging toggle.

- New 'Video capture method' setting: Canvas 2D (default) or WebGL for hardware-overlay platforms like WebOS where Canvas 2D returns black pixels.
- New 'Send debug logs to mock WLED server' checkbox, off by default.

Fix: LEDs silent on WebOS/hardware-overlay platforms.

New setting: Video capture method. Select WebGL if the LEDs do not turn on during playback (hardware overlay issue on WebOS/smart TV platforms).
New setting: Send debug logs to mock WLED server (keeps mock log clean by default).
Add plugin icon (shown in Jellyfin's plugin catalogue).

First stable release.

Full-featured WLED edge-lighting plugin for Jellyfin:
- Direct browser → WLED WebSocket connection
- Auto-detects letterbox/pillarbox bars; samples only the actual picture area
- Per-device restriction: limit edge lighting to one specific Jellyfin client
- Inline connection test against the live URL input value
- Clockwise/counter-clockwise strip direction, configurable start position
- Adjustable brightness, sample depth, and update rate
